Revvity is a science-based solutions company that leverages innovation across life sciences and diagnostics to improve lives everywhere. The company serves a diverse customer base, including academia, pharma/biotech, clinical laboratories, and healthcare professionals. Its extensive range of products and services includes genomic analysis, clinical diagnostics, cell and gene therapy, drug discovery, precision medicine, and advanced automation solutions. Revvity is committed to empowering scientists with cutting-edge tools and comprehensive solutions to enhance research and development, diagnostics, and clinical testing. With a focus on accelerating time to market while ensuring quality, Revvity is dedicated to revolutionizing next-generation breakthroughs in healthcare and life sciences.

highlights:

# Full-Stack AI Developer Intern

Remote, USA

Posted Jun 5, 2026

Full-time

## Job Title Full-Stack AI Developer Intern Location(s) CAD Remote – ON About Us Revvity is a developer and provider of end-to-end solutions designed to help scientists, researchers, and clinicians solve the world’s greatest health challenges. We pair the enthusiasm of an industry disruptor with the experience of a longtime leader. Our team of 11,000+ colleagues from around the globe are vital to our success and the reason we’re able to push boundaries in pursuit of better human health. Find your future at Revvity We are looking for a forward-thinking AI Intern to join our HR and AI Teams here at Revvity. This person will play a pivotal role in the end-to-end lifecycle of our HR AI initiatives - from initial data preparation to live deployment and engagement analysis. This role is a unique blend of Technical Development, Data Engineering, and Strategic Pilot Support, offering a 360-degree view of how AI is reshaping the modern workplace. Key Responsibilities: 1. Agent Lifecycle & Optimization Support Development:Assist in building and refining existing and new HR AI Agents Fine-Tuning & Testing:Conduct rigorous testing and iterative fine-tuning to ensure agent responses are accurate, compliant, and empathetic. Dataset Engineering:Lead the preparation, cleaning, and labeling of high-quality training data to improve agent "intelligence" and reduce bias. 2. AI Engagement & Admin AI Engagement:Contribute to and supportthe AI at RevvitySpace on RISE, AI User Clubs, and AI Month events by assisting with workshops, prompting contests, and preparing live AI demos 3. Research & Innovation Critical Thinking Pilot:Provide dedicated support to our "Critical Thinking Pilot" by assisting in the creation of a research bot within RevvityAI to conduct studies, focusing primarily on the "AI-enabled performance tasks“ Qualifications & Skills: Technical Base:Familiarity with Python, LLM prompting, or No-Code/Low-Code AI agent builders. Analytical Mindset:Strong data curation skills and an eye for detail (essential for training data and testing). Critical Thinker: Ability to question AI outputs and analyze the ethical implications of automated systems. 

Apply To This Job

[...]
Revvity is a developer and provider of end-to-end solutions designed to help scientists, researchers, and clinicians solve the world’s greatest health challenges. We pair the enthusiasm of an industry disruptor with the experience of a longtime leader. Our team of 11,000+ colleagues from around the globe are vital to our success and the reason we’re able to push boundaries in pursuit of better human health.
[...]
- Revvity Signals makes market-leading software that empowers scientists across Life Science R&D, clinical research, and specialty chemicals to make better medicines and products, faster. Our flagship offering is the SaaS Signals Research Platform that provides knowledge capture, collaboration, analysis and visualization tools across the full depth and breadth of scientific discovery workflows. The viral adoption of our software is accelerating innovation, expediting drug development for a healthier world and facilitating the creation of sustainable materials.
[...]
- Join us as a Lead AI Software Architect/Engineer - where you will manage the development of AI processes, workflows, models and applications to transform the organization into a high-performance AI first organization.
[...]
Key Responsibilities:
[...]
- Technical Architecture & Development -
- Design and develop greenfield AI processes, agentic workflows, and scalable AI infrastructure
- Lead cross-functional engineering teams and mentor developers
- Build large-scale, high-availability AI applications using modern programming languages (Python, Java, Go)
- Collaborate on platform services integration across Revvity products

- AI Transformation & Enablement -
- Partner with functional teams to identify and implement high-leverage AI workflow transformations
- Build custom tools, agents, and automations tailored to specific business needs
- Coach teams through progressive AI adoption: awareness → first wins → full transformation → self-sufficiency
- Document and scale successful patterns across the organization
[...]
- Basic Qualifications:
[...]
- BS/MS Computer Science or equivalent
- 5-10+ years software engineering/architecture experience
- 5+ years hands-on AI/ML infrastructure and LLM application development

- Preferred Qualifications:
[...]
- Expert-level Python and Java programming
- Proven experience with distributed frameworks (Dask, Spark, Ray) and AWS
- Demonstrated success building AI tools that transformed real work processes
- Strong coaching/teaching abilities with evidence of enabling others
[...]
- What Makes You Successful:
[...]
- You've personally lived the AI transformation you'll drive for others
- You build agents and automations fluently, not just theoretically
- You're an exceptional communicator who adapts to different learning styles
- You recognize patterns and think in scalable systems
- Your bias toward action - showing working prototypes over theoretical presentations

results.title: Senior DevOps Engineer | Hirevector
highlights:
# Senior DevOps Engineer
[...]
, 2026
[...]
- Senior DevOps Engineer
[...]
- Location(s)
[...]
Revvity is a developer and provider of end-to-end solutions designed to help scientists, researchers, and clinicians solve the world’s greatest health challenges. We pair the enthusiasm of an industry disruptor with the experience of a longtime leader. Our team of 11,000+ colleagues from around the globe are vital to our success and the reason we’re able to push boundaries in pursuit of better human health.
[...]
Revvity Signals makes market-leading software that empowers scientists across Life Science R&D, clinical research, and specialty chemicals to make better medicines and products, faster. Our flagship offering is the SaaS Signals Research Platform that provides knowledge capture, collaboration, analysis and visualization tools across the full depth and breadth of scientific discovery workflows. The viral adoption of our software is accelerating innovation, expediting drug development for a healthier world and facilitating the creation of sustainable materials.
[...]
Join us as a Senior DevOps Engineer (AI/ML), where you'll play a pivotal role building out, maintaining and operating our AI infrastructure, that is part of the Signals Platform. This is your chance to work closely with a dedicated team of experience developers building out AI capabilities within our product.
[...]
We are seeking a senior-level technical expert working within the R&D organization, who integrates and orchestrates the entire lifecycle of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) projects.
[...]
You will be responsible for bridging the gap between AI researchers, and traditional software development teams to ensure AI models are reliably built, tested, and deployed into production at scale.
[...]
This role requires a blend of deep expertise in DevOps principles, software engineering, and AI/ML infrastructure, with a strong focus on automation, scalability, and security.
[...]
- Architect, design, and lead the implementation of scalable, secure, and resilient infrastructure for AI/ML workloads

- Build and maintain the continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipelines specifically for AI models, managing everything from data ingestion to model serving and monitoring

- Act as a subject matter expert and mentor for junior engineers, driving the adoption of best practices for AI/ML platform development, code management, and deployment

- Work closely with AI researchers, and software engineers to optimize performance and streamline the end-to-end development and experimentation process.

- Ensure the reliability, security, and performance of AI systems.

- Stay ahead of industry trends and bring innovative AI solutions to the table.
[...]
Basic Qualifications:
[...]
- 5+ years of experience in DevOps, ML Infrastructure, or Platform Engineering, with several years in a senior or leadership capacity.

- 5+ years of deep, expert-level knowledge of at least one major cloud provider (e.g., AWS, Azure, or GCP), including their AI/ML and containerization services.

- 5+ years of hands-on experience with ML orchestration and platform tools like Kubeflow, MLflow, or Amazon SageMaker.

- 5+ years of deep expertise with Docker and Kubernetes for managing and scaling AI/ML workloads.

- 5+ years strong coding skills in languages such as Python, Go, or Bash for automation.
[...]
Preferred Qualifications:
[...]
- Proficiency with tools like Terraform or Ansible to manage and provision infrastructure.

- Experience building robust and automated CI/CD pipelines using tools like GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, or Jenkins.

- Excellent collaboration and communication skills to work effectively with cross-functional technical and business teams.

results.title: Senior DevOps Engineer | Jobflarely
highlights: About Us Revvity is a developer and provider of end-to-end solutions designed to help scientists, researchers, and clinicians solve the world’s greatest health challenges. We pair the enthusiasm of an industry disruptor with the experience of a longtime leader. Our team of 11,000+ colleagues from around the globe are vital to our success and the reason we’re able to push boundaries in pursuit of better human health. Find your future at Revvity Revvity Signals makes market-leading software that empowers scientists across Life Science R&D, clinical research, and specialty chemicals to make better medicines and products, faster. Our flagship offering is the SaaS Signals Research Platform that provides knowledge capture, collaboration, analysis and visualization tools across the full depth and breadth of scientific discovery workflows. The viral adoption of our software is accelerating innovation, expediting drug development for a healthier world and facilitating the creation of sustainable materials. Join us as a Senior DevOps Engineer (AI/ML) , where you'll play a pivotal role building out, maintaining and operating our AI infrastructure, that is part of the Signals Platform. This is your chance to work closely with a dedicated team of experience developers building out AI capabilities within our product. We are seeking a senior-level technical expert working within the R&D organization, who integrates and orchestrates the entire lifecycle of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) projects. You will be responsible for bridging the gap between AI researchers, and traditional software development teams to ensure AI models are reliably built, tested, and deployed into production at scale. This role requires a blend of deep expertise in DevOps principles, software engineering, and AI/ML infrastructure, with a strong focus on automation, scalability, and security. Main functions Architect, design, and lead the implementation of scalable, secure, and resilient infrastructure for AI/ML workloads Build and maintain the continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipelines specifically for AI models, managing everything from data ingestion to model serving and monitoring Act as a subject matter expert and mentor for junior engineers, driving the adoption of best practices for AI/ML platform development, code management, and deployment Work closely with AI researchers, and software engineers to optimize performance and streamline the end-to-end development and experimentation process. Ensure the reliability, security, and performance of AI systems. Stay ahead of industry trends and bring innovative AI solutions to the table. Basic Qualifications: 5+ years of experience in DevOps, ML Infrastructure, or Platform Engineering, with several years in a senior or leadership capacity. 5+ years of deep, expert-level knowledge of at least one major cloud provider (e.g., AWS, Azure, or GCP), including their AI/ML and containerization services. 5+ years of hands-on experience with ML orchestration and platform tools like Kubeflow, MLflow, or Amazon SageMaker. 5+ years of deep expertise with Docker and Kubernetes for managing and scaling AI/ML workloads. 5+ years strong coding skills in languages such as Python, Go, or Bash for automation. Preferred Qualifications: Proficiency with tools like Terraform or Ansible to manage and provision infrastructure. Experience building robust and automated CI/CD pipelines using tools like GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, or Jenkins. Excellent collaboration and communication skills to work effectively with cross-functional technical and business teams. The working hours for this position will be EST. Candidates must have the capability to Interview In-Person at our Headquarters in Waltham, MA USA. Please include a valid LinkedIn profile and added work links, to your resume. Fraud Prevention Notice : We are committed to a fair and transparent hiring process. All applicants must provide

Revvity is a developer and provider of end-to-end solutions designed to help scientists, researchers, and clinicians solve the world’s greatest health challenges. We pair the enthusiasm of an industry disruptor with the experience of a longtime leader. Our team of 11,000+ colleagues from around the globe are vital to our success and the reason we’re able to push boundaries in pursuit of better human health.
[...]
We are looking for a talented Principal Software Development Engineer (Scala, Big Data Engineering, SQL/NoSQL) to join our Signals Software family and to lead the Back End team building a product we call the Signals Data Factory.
[...]
The Signals Data Factory is a product which specializes in taking in many different forms of scientific data, transforming it, and making it searchable (using Elasticsearch) and analyzable via a variety of interfaces. Its current use spans from early in the drug-discovery process to analyzing active clinical trial data. If you are looking to join a team of talented engineers and to contribute to delivering amazing, high impact, customer-focused features, this is a great opportunity.
[...]
- You Will:
- Build and maintain new, high impact functionality into the Signals Data Factory
- Work on a medium to large data transformation engine
- Work on a system capable of processing petabytes of data
- Work with AWS services and cloud technologies
- Create secure, performant interfaces and services
- Craft well-documented, reusable, and testable code
- Guide fellow team members on priorities, specifications, and feature implementation
[...]
- Basic Qualifications:
- 7+ years of Software Development experience
- 7+ years of ​Enterprise Level Application Design experience
- 7+ years of Big Data Processing and Transformation Pipelines expertise
- 7+ years of demonstrated Leadership Qualities, to technically guide others
- 7+ years expertise with Scala
- 7+ years of experienced with Relational Database Design and Development
- 7+ years skilled with Mongo DB, Elasticsearch and Spark
[...]
- Preferred Qualifications:
[...]
- Bachelor's Degree is preferred
- Skilled with building APIs in Node.js
- Kubernetes, Rancher & Docker experience
- Experience with cloud-hosting at one of the top 3 providers
- A good understanding of git workflows is needed
- Some experience with job scheduling workflows
- Strongly driven – ability to clarify requirements and deliver code in a highly distributed and fast-moving environment

results.title: Sr/ Bioinformatics Analyst /REMOTE/ | Jobflarely
highlights:
Revvity is a developer and provider of end-to-end solutions designed to help scientists, researchers, and clinicians solve the world’s greatest health challenges. We pair the enthusiasm of an industry disruptor with the experience of a longtime leader. Our team of 11,000+ colleagues from around the globe are vital to our success and the reason we’re able to push boundaries in pursuit of better human health. Find your future at Revvity Your New Adventure: Bioinformatics Analyst – Revvity Signals Are you a lab scientist ready to leverage your hands-on research experience in a dynamic customer-facing role? This position is perfect for bench scientists seeking to transition their deep laboratory expertise into direct client engagement. You'll use your real-world lab experience to understand customer challenges, provide technical solutions, and bridge the gap between complex scientific instrumentation and practical laboratory applications. The Big Picture As a Bioinformatics Analyst, you'll lead the implementation and configuration of Revvity's Signals One solutions for our enterprise clients. You'll work at the intersection of science and technology, helping organizations transform their scientific workflows through our cutting-edge digital platforms. What's in it for You • Experience personalized career journeys with growth-oriented development opportunities. • Engage in purpose-driven work that makes a positive impact on society. • Be part of a values-driven culture that fosters inclusivity and innovation. Your Superpowers in Action • Lead or support the deployment of Revvity's Signals One solutions, customizing and configuring the platform to align with customer-specific scientific workflows. • Collaborate with customers to gather requirements, analyze scientific processes, and translate them into effective system configurations. • Provide strategic guidance and best practices throughout the implementation lifecycle, building strong relationships with stakeholders as a trusted advisor. • Deliver training sessions and provide ongoing support to ensure successful adoption and usage of the platform. • Domestic and International Travel: 5-10% Education Qualifications • Bachelors level degree in Bioinformatics, Computational Biology, Biotechnology, Chemistry, Biology, or a related scientific discipline. • AND a minimum of 5 years of relevant experience in research or clinical data analysis and related techniques in the field of drug discovery, epidemiology, biostatistics. Preferred Qualifications • Experience with various InVivo and Invitro Molecular Biology techniques like ELISA, Flow Cytometry, other high throughput screening assays, and Mass Spectroscopy. • Experience with high throughput screening results utilizing enterprise level bioinformatics software to carry out structure activity analyses and to generate predictive correlations. • Experience with Antibody Drug Conjugate studies, computational analysis of therapeutic proteins, antibodies, or biosimilars from discovery through development. • Ability to consult with clients to convey best approach to achieve visual analytics for scientific concepts. • Scientific fluency and a demonstrated ability to work cross-functionally with an emphasis on teamwork, collaboration, and communication to create and launch innovative, successful solutions. • Experience with Spotfire, Research Informatics, ELN, & LIMS platforms preferred • Excellent verbal, written communication, and interpersonal skills • Confidence in your ability to face customers in a fast paced, growth environment is needed. • Experience with analysis packages and programming languages such as Python, R, SQL a plus • Strong initiative, with strengths in problem-solving, issue-resolution, ability to work in an ambiguous and deadline-driven work environment, and ability to multitask. #LI-JB1 Original job Sr/ Bioinformatics Analyst /REMOTE/ posted on GrabJobs

Apply for This Job

Revvity is a developer and provider of end-to-end solutions designed to help scientists, researchers, and clinicians solve the world’s greatest health challenges. We pair the enthusiasm of an industry disruptor with the experience of a longtime leader. Our team of 11,000+ colleagues from around the globe are vital to our success and the reason we’re able to push boundaries in pursuit of better human health.

Join Revvity as a USCAN Commercial Senior Financial Analyst and become the strategic finance partner driving commercial success across our Platform and Reagents Segments in North America. Your expertise in product margin analysis, pricing dynamics, and volume trends will be instrumental in optimizing our portfolio performance and driving sustainable profitability across key business segments.

- Other tasks as directed by the USCAN Director of Finance and Business Leadership.

- A minimum of 3 years of corporate finance experience at a multi-national, matrixed organization OR 5 years of corporate experience

- Experience in a Financial Leadership Development Program

Generative AI - Senior Associate Job Opening in New York, New York - Crains New York

Generative AI - Senior Associate

Generative AI - Senior Associate
[...]
Generative artificial intelligence is transforming how we build products, serve clients, and operate at scale. In the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help turn advanced models into dependable, secure, and high-performing production services. You will work with partners across machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering to deliver solutions with clear return on investment. If you enjoy hands-on engineering, real-world constraints, and high-impact delivery, this role is for you. As a Senior Associate, Generative AI Engineer in the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help design, build, and support production generative artificial intelligence products and reusable backend application programming interfaces used across the firm. You will combine large enterprise datasets with large language and multimodal models to deliver scalable, measurable solutions. You will collaborate closely with machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering partners to ensure reliability, performance, and strong operational controls. You will contribute to technical design decisions, delivery planning, and continuous improvement of our platforms and products.Job responsibilities

- PhD in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science

[...]

[...]
- Strong software engineering

development lifecycle best practices

[...]
- Strong problem-framing skills to align machine learning solutions to business objectives and constraints

Preferred qualifications, capabilities
[...]
- Experience designing and implementing pipeline workflows using directed acyclic graph frameworks (for example Kubeflow, DVC, or Ray)
[...]
- Experience building batch and streaming microservices exposed via gRPC and/or GraphQL
[...]
- Demonstrable experience with parameter-efficient fine-tuning, quantization, and quantization-aware fine-tuning for large language models

[...]
- Experience with multimodal large language model use cases (text plus image, speech, or video)
